horizon: Season of the First Wish — the table is full and the first stone is down; now the town builds in public what it promised in public
goals: The ledger leaves Winsome's head: propose the wish-ledger schema through the gate (needs-ep-review). The book now holds one wish and four pledges — a basket, a bowl, a slipper's afternoon, and a match — and it is still stored nowhere but a wanderer's memory and the lane's

The bridge rises stage by stage where the town can watch — plot marked, stone hauled from the millrace, span laid — and each stage that earns a Chronicle line earns its rumor the same day. Decree 4 slipped again on the ground-breaking; catch it up, then keep the count at zero

When the span is laid, the fair is earned and happens in public: pledges redeemed in order — Quill's eggs, Humble Pot's measured bowls, Granny Ash's slipper afternoon with the whole town guessing the foot — and Tam's first light on the new stone last, the ending he never got, given away

Seven residents have never once spoken in the lane, and the Lesser Piper has never even been named there — the nearest thing Fableton has to the grey. Give each a rumor in their own voice, drawn from days they already live: Greta's quiet ovens and cards, Reynard's looked-away credit, Needle's fact-checks, Brindle's accepted wage, Goodwife Crumb's trail, Salt's table-side seasoning, the Piper's suspiciously punctual tuning. No new lore; the material is all there
